resources.string: This is a real-world, real-time, location based game. Take care whilst playing, and always be aware of hazards around you.
Other players will be able to see your real-life location. By playing, you agree to allow other players to see your real-life location.
Playing this game whilst driving, operating machinery, cycling, or whilst in command of air or water craft may be illegal and dangerous.
Observe all local laws, and do not engage in unlawful trespass or other criminal activity.
You are solely responsible for your decision to play this game, and any consequences that may arise from doing so.

Counterforce is a free game that does not engage in any marketing whatsoever, and personal information will be treated with utmost respect. Policy changes will always be by prior agreement in order to continue playing, should Counterforce ever become a marketable product.

A unique, securely-encrypted key derived from your device\'s identity is used for the sole purpose of identifying your account. Other non-personally-identifying parameters of the device, and lists of running apps/services, may be accessed to prevent cheating.

Your real world location will be shared with other players. A \"Privacy Zone\" feature exists which allows you to obscure sensitive locations such as home and workplaces from the game. It is advisable to make use of this facility before you begin playing, by clicking the icon on the registration screen or accessing it in the Settings menu later. It bears the following symbol:

All in-game activity is logged on the server for administration, debugging and development purposes. Cooperation with the UK authorities (or their nominated parties) will be made in the highly unlikely event that they should wish to access the data for the purposes of law enforcement, security or national interests.
No making threats of violence against other players.
No pornographic or offensive names or avatars.
Alert the admins of any glitches or exploits you discover.
Making multiple accounts, including by uninstalling and reinstalling the game, is forbidden.
I agree to the above, that I am solely responsible for my own actions and choice to play this game, and will exercise common sense when doing so.
A unique identity could not be determined for this device, because it is either a non-cellular tablet, emulator, unsupported phone, or the "Telephony"/"Read Phone State" permission was not enabled.
Unfortunately, only cellular phones (or other devices that at least take a SIM card) are supported.

Emulator use is strictly forbidden. Registering an account with one will result in a permanent ban not only for that account, but for all devices that are determined to have shared the same IP or geographical address too! DO NOT CONTINUE if this is the case. Press \"Quit\".

If you are using a regular cellular phone and thus getting this message in error, please \"Quit\", check that all permissions required by this app are enabled in the app settings, and try again. Please e-mail the developer or report to the Discord group if this does not fix the problem.
Equip this system to fire missiles from your location.
Equip this system to intercept enemy missiles from your location.
Missile launch sites fire cruise missiles, allowing you to attack other players in your region.
Established at %1$s accuracy, but must be better than %2$s. Searching for better...
You cannot build here as you are too close to other structures.
You cannot build an ore mine here as all ore in the vicinity is already being mined.\nYou must be at least %1$s from:
Your online structures cost %1$s per hour.
Your maintenance costs exceed your wealth generation.
Using %1$s location (%2$.6f, %3$.6f) with accuracy %4$s from %5$s ago.
The following players wish to join %1$s
You have no available missiles in range of this target.
You have no available interceptors in range of this target.
Click on the map to define a new privacy zone, or click existing zones to edit them.
Your player is currently within this privacy zone. It will become effective only once your player has moved outside of it.
You are in a privacy zone. Using your last location outside of this zone.
Please register a username and avatar:
Please specify an alliance name, description and avatar:
Your alliance is at war with this alliance.
You have requested to join this alliance, and are waiting for a leader to approve or decline.
Alliance name taken. Please specify another.
A major software update has been released.
The update must be installed before you can log in again. It should be available to all regions within a few hours.
You must specify a target. Touch locations or entities on the map to designate them as a target first.
Target out of range. You may only designate a target within the range circle.
Wilful friendly fire is not allowed.
Your interceptor cannot intercept the selected missile.
Waiting for data. Please try again in a moment.
You must respawn before you can do this!
Players cannot carry nuclear weapons. You must build a missile battery.
Players cannot carry ICBMs. You must build an ICBM Silo.
You must build an ICBM Silo to purchase ICBMs.
The system is reloading and cannot yet fire.
This missile is preparing and cannot yet fire.
The system is offline and cannot fire.
Decommission %1$s for %2$s refund? (this will take %3$s to complete)

Are you sure you want to close your account?\n\nYour player will be removed from the game and all your structures will be deleted immediately. This action cannot be undone! Once again, are you sure you want to delete your player and all your structures and units?
Construct an %1$s at your location for %2$s?
Switch to auto?\n\nIn auto mode, threats will be engaged automatically at any time, regardless of whether you\'re online or alive.
Switch to semi auto?\n\nIn semi auto mode, threats will only be automatically be engaged when you are offline or dead. You will have control whenever you are online and alive.
Switch to manual?\n\nIn manual mode, threats will not be engaged automatically. You will have full control.
Request to join %1$s? This will cancel your request to join %2$s.
Leave %1$s? You will have to wait %2$s before you can join or create another alliance.
Leave %1$s? As you are the only leader, this will disband the alliance. You will have to wait %2$s before you can join or create another alliance.
Promote %1$s to be a leader of %2$s? This promotion will be permanent until they leave.
You are in the cooloff period from leaving your previous alliance, and must wait %1$s before you can create or join another.
You are dead. You may respawn in %s.
Use two fingers to set the avatar image bounds.
Please select the avatar image bounds.
Upgrade the launcher from %1$d slots to %2$d for %3$s?
Upgrade the launcher from %1$d slots to %2$d for %3$s? (Long-press to upgrade all the way to the max at once.)
Upgrade the reload system from %1$s to %2$s for %3$s?
Upgrade to take nuclear missiles for %1$s?

Bring %1$s online?

It will cost %2$s per hour to run.

It will take %3$s to boot.

Take %1$s offline?

You will save %2$s per hour.

It takes %3$s to bring it back online.
You will heal naturally in %1$s. Heal fully now for %2$s?
You will die of radiation poisoning unless you leave the radioactive area or it dissipates. Heal fully now for %1$s?
You are already at full health.
You are dead and cannot heal.
Build a SAM battery or buy an air defence system to protect yourself from threats.
Build a missile battery or buy a cruise missile system.
Your structures will spontaneously go offline if you can\'t afford the hourly costs.
You can\'t do anything and aren\'t generating income. Respawn as soon as possible.
Radiation slowly drains your HP. If you can\'t leave, you should heal yourself periodically.
Hourly wealth generation stops above %1$s. Spend some money!
%1$s is much more powerful than you. Attacking them will likely result in a devastating retaliation. Continue?
%1$s is respawn protected. Them and their semi-transparent structures are currently invulnerable for %2$s. Continue?
You are currently respawn protected, and will be invulnerable for %1$s. Launching a missile will remove this invulnerability now. Continue?
Nuclear missiles create a radioactive area that damages players, and a large EMP pulse that can knock structures offline.
This missile will follow targeted players, trucks, tanks, and infantry, even if they move. This missile CANNOT track ships or submarines.
ICBMs carry powerful warheads around the globe at extreme speeds and can only be intercepted by Anti-Ballistic Missiles.
Long-press to reset this player\'s avatar.
This missile is too fast to be engaged by some interceptor types.
Welcome, commander. Would you like a tutorial?
Please specify a valid username. It must contain valid, visible characters and comply with the game rules.
Accept affiliation from %1$s? This will last until %2$s.
Your alliance is affiliated with this alliance.
An affiliation proposal exists between your alliance and this one.
The following alliances wish to affiliate with %1$s
Alternatively, press \"Generate Identity\" to create a restricted, non-supported and non-recoverable tablet account.
Reject affiliation proposal and declare war on %1$s? This war will last until the end of the week (%2$s) or until either alliance disbands.
Counterforce requires the following permissions to be enabled:
Counterforce is a multiplayer online game, and therefore requires internet access.
TCP/IP is required for Counterforce to communicate with the game server, which this permission enables.
Counterforce offers optional vibration to accompany \"under attack\" warnings.
Configuration files and other game elements are cached on your device for efficiency. Counterforce must be able to read and write to storage to facilitate this.
Counterforce is a location-based game that uses your device\'s GPS to place your character in the game world. The \"fine location\" permission is required for this to work.
Your Counterforce account requires a unique identity, which is securely derived from your device\'s IMEI\/MEID. The \"read phone state\" permission enables this information to be retreived.
Counterforce raises optional \"under attack\" notifications. This permission enables the threat checking service to automatically re-enable itself after a reboot.
Press the button below to show prompts asking you to enable the permissions. If this doesn\'t work, you will need to enable the permissions in Android Settings \> Apps \> Counterforce \> Permissions
You will receive warnings that you or your allies are under attack.
Sentry guns provide short range defence against missiles and aircraft.
Extractors more efficiently produce resources from nearby deposits. The type of nearby deposit will automatically determine what type of extractor is constructed.
This area is already being mined. Further online ore mines will significantly reduce the quantity of resources discovered!

Counterforce is struggling to determine your location. Please ensure your device\'s GPS is switched on, with high accuracy enabled. If you\'ve already done this, try taking your device outside or near a window.
A general error occurred. Please contact an administrator.
The new connection settings were not applied as they were invalid.
You cannot leave an alliance while in battle!
%s is still in battle and cannot be kicked!
You cannot join an alliance while in battle!
%s is still in battle and cannot be accepted yet!
You cannot create an alliance while respawn protected. Your respawn protection ends in %1$s.
Notification sound & vibration are now part of Apps & Notifications in Android settings.
For being alive. This amount will increase by 50 every time you rank up.
Join or create an alliance, that has an avatar.
Possess a defence system consisting of at least one online structure.

Having a name that broke the game rules

Radar Stations scan within an 800 km radius to uncover hostile missiles, aircraft, and ground units.
Upgrade the accuracy bonus range from %1$s to %2$s for %3$s?
You have no online radar stations! Build one or turn one online so you can see the whole map.
No radar coverage in this area!
Anti-Ballistic Missile Silos hold interceptor missiles that can shoot down incoming ICBMs, protecting you from certain destruction.
This object is beyond your radar range. Move closer or activate a radar station to see it.
This target is beyond your radar range. Move closer or activate a nearby radar station to attack it.
This interceptor can reliably destroy ICBMs and will only automatically fire at ICBMs.
This interceptor is fast enough to intercept any non-ICBM missile.
Upgrade the radar accuracy bonus from %1$s to %2$s for %3$s?
Radar stations give an accuracy bonus to structures that are inside the blue circle around the radar station.
Sentry guns offer close-quarters protection to you and your structures.

SAM batteries store interceptors that destroy incoming missiles. By default, SAM batteries offer no accuracy bonus to their interceptors. Build a radar station near by to increase the accuracy bonus of the SAM battery, which will be added to interceptors launched from this SAM battery. The SAM will not fire on anything beyond its engagement distance, shown as an orange circle on the map. Tap the engagement distance button to set a custom range.
Missile batteries hold missiles you can use to attack your enemies. They can be upgraded to a maximum capacity of 126 missile slots.
Ore mines extract resources from resource deposits.
SAM batteries store interceptor missiles and engage enemy cruise missiles with them, protecting you and your assets.
Gun turrets fire artillery shells at ground targets, for base defence or lighter close-range assaults.
Upgrade the sentry range from %1$s to %2$s for %3$s?

No radar coverage in the area. Targets unknown.
No radar coverage in the area. Target unknown.

Switch ICBM silo to manual? In manual mode, the silo will not retaliate.
CommandPosts protect nearby players from missiles and radiation.
When the owner is near by, farms will produce valuable crops players can go collect.

Bombers have long ranges and carry air-launched missiles and bombs.
Fighters carry interceptors used to engage hostile aircraft and missiles.
Stealth fighters carry interceptors and are harder to detect on radar.
Stealth bombers carry missiles long distances and are harder to detect on radar.
AWACs carry airborne radars that can detect hostile aircraft, missiles, ground units, and ships.
Attack aircraft carry missiles and are equipped with a cannon for attacking ground targets.
Aerial refuelers allow the mid-air refueling of friendly aircraft for extended range missions.
Cargo planes transport resources and ground units.
Supersonic bombers are significantly faster than normal bombers, but carry fewer missiles.
Multi-role aircraft carry interceptors and missiles for engaging ground and airborne targets.
ICBM silos hold powerful nuclear ICBMs that can bring devastation to far-away enemies.

This interceptor has a nuclear warhead. It has a 100% chance to destroy its target, and a smaller chance to destroy other missiles/aircraft in the blast radius.
This torpedo is armed with a nuclear warhead. It will deal severe damage to any vessel within a large radius.

You must wait %1$s before prospecting again.

Airdrops can be called to bring the player supplies. They can be called every %1$s and carry a value of %2$s - %3$s.
Public designs can be purchased by anyone. Otherwise, the design will only be available to you and the people in your alliance.
Allows the aircraft to land/take-off from aircraft carriers. Limits aircraft weight to %1$s.
Adds a sentrygun-like cannon to the aircraft, allowing it to shoot down other aircraft and missiles. Adds %1$s to aircraft weight.

Allows the aircraft to use its cannon to attack ground structures and units. WARNING: ground attack aircraft can be destroyed by sentry-guns and hit more easily by interceptors. Adds %1$s to aircraft weight.
Adds an electronic warfare suite to the aircraft, which can be used to disable structures and interceptors. Adds %1$s to aircraft weight.
Adds mid-air refueling equipment to the aircraft, allowing it to refuel other aircraft in-flight. Adds %1$s to aircraft weight.
Adds a radar to the aircraft, allowing it to detect ships. Adds %1$s to aircraft weight.
Adds stealth to the aircraft. Stealth missiles and aircraft cannot be automatically engaged with interceptors unless they are within %1$s. Multiplies final aircraft price by %2$s.
Adds missiles to the aircraft. Each missile adds %1$s to aircraft weight. Max %2$s.
Adds interceptors to the aircraft. Each interceptor adds %1$s to aircraft weight. Max %2$s.
Adds flares to the aircraft. Flares give the aircraft a better chance to evade enemy interceptors. Each flare adds %1$s to aircraft weight. Max %2$s.
The aircraft\'s fuel tank, in metric tons. Each metric ton of fuel capacity adds another metric ton for fuel-tank weight.
In metric tons. Allows the aircraft to carry resources or infantry.
In mach. Higher speed results in less fuel efficiency and shorter range. Increasing speed greatly increases final price.
In metric tons. Aircraft HP is based on aircraft weight. Adding more weight in armor therefore increases HP.
Allows the aircraft to carry nuclear weapons. Adds %1$s to final price.

Your design must have a range of at least %1$s. Increase the fuel capacity or decrease weight.

Electronic warfare can be used to disable structures and destroy interceptor missiles within %s. Aircraft equipped with electronic warfare are considered dangerous and will be fired upon by non-allied SAMs.
Infantry units capture structures and can be used to fight other infantry units.
Tanks can attack structures, tanks, and cargo trucks.

Select a captured city, structure, or player to liberate it. If the original owner of a structure or city is captured, you need to liberate them first.

At full capacity. Cannot load more resources.
Cargo trucks pick up and transport cargo for the player.
Mining trucks have far worse fuel efficiency and weigh far more than a cargo truck, but also haul drastically larger amounts of cargo.
You can only deliver cargo to friendly units.
Select an aircraft in the airbase to transfer cargo to. (Load fuel into the airbase by selecting the airbase.)
The unit you have selected cannot receive this type of cargo.
Use %1$s oil to refuel this vessel?
Tap "yes" to load the oil as cargo. Press "no" to use it to refuel the ship.
The selected unit has a resource system (for using or processing resources) and a cargo bay. Would you like to load into the cargo bay? (Tap "no" to load the resources for use or processing.)
The selected unit has a missile launch system and a cargo bay. Would you like to load into the cargo cargo bay? (Tap "no" to load the missiles into the launcher.)
Touch a loot container to load it into the %s.
You must select loot to load.
Prospecting allows the player to discover nearby resource deposits.
You must build ore mines near a resource deposit.
Processors convert one or multiple types of resources into more advanced resources.
Granaries refine crop resources into food.
Foundries refine coal and ore into metal.
Oil refineries refine crude oil into fuel.
Construction yards refine concrete and lumber into construction materials.
Explosives factories refine saltpeter and coal into explosives used for missile production.
Chemical plants refine saltpeter and oil into nerve agent used in the production of chemical weapons.
Power plants consume coal and produce electricity.
Nuclear power plants consume fissile material and produce large amounts of electricity.
Enrichment facilities consume uranium and produce fissile material for use in nuclear power plants and to construct nuclear weapons.
Machine shops consume metal and oil to produce machinery.
Laboratories consume food and oil and produce medicine.
Lithography plants consume electricity and machinery and produce electronics.
Universities consume labor and produce knowledge.
Ethanol plants consume crops and produce fuel.
Charcoal factories consume fuel and lumber and produce coal.
The structure must be online to do this.
Use the plus and minus buttons to specify the quantity of %s you would like to drop, then press OK.
The vessel\'s fuel tank, in metric tons. Each metric ton of fuel capacity adds another metric ton for fuel-tank weight.
In metric tons. Vessel HP is based on displacement. Adding more weight in armor therefore increases HP.
Adds missiles to the vessel. Each missile adds %1$s metric tons to vessel displacement. Max %2$s.
Adds an artillery gun to the vessel. Each gun adds %1$s metric tons to vessel displacement and %2$s artillery shell slots. Max %3$s. Each artillery gun added decreases the reload time.
Adds torpedoes to the vessel. Each torpedo adds %1$s metric tons to vessel displacement. Max %2$s.
Adds ICBMs to the submarine. Each ICBM adds %1$s metric tons to submarine displacement. Max %2$s.
Adds nuclear propulsion to the vessel. Adds %1$s to vessel cost and %2$s to vessel displacement. Grants infinite range.
Adds radar to the ship, allowing it to detect other ships and aircraft at extended range. Adds %1$s to vessel displacement.
Adds active sonar to the ship, allowing it to detect submarines at extended ranges. Adds %1$s to vessel displacement.
Adds air-defense missiles to the vessel. Each interceptor adds %1$s metric tons to vessel displacement. Max %2$s.
Adds cargo capacity to the vessel.
Adds hangar space to the vessel, allowing it to host aircraft.
Adds air defense guns to the vessel that can engage aircraft and missiles.
Allows the vessel to load and unload ground units in the water. Otherwise, it can only load and unload at ports or shipyards. Increases vessel weight by %s.
Allows nearby ships to refuel and repair as if they were in port. Note: support ships cannot perform this function for each other! Increases vessel weight by %s.
This vessel has nuclear propulsion, giving it infinite range.
This vessel is equipped with sonar, allowing it to detect submarines at extended range.
This vessel is equipped with radar, allowing it to detect aircraft and ships at extended range.
Submarines are equipped with sonar, can hide underwater, and can be custom-designed to carry missiles, torpedoes, and SLBMs.
Ships can be custom-designed by the player and can perform a wide range tasks including transporting cargo, aircraft, ship-to-ship combat, and more.
This missile will track ships and surfaced submarines, even if they move, and deal extra damage to naval targets.
This missile can damage submerged submarines.
You cannot do this while the submarine is submerged.
The vessel must be within %s of a friendly shipyard or port to re-arm.
The vessel is not within %s of a friendly shipyard or port. Prep times for missiles are extended.
You cannot sell units while in battle.
The vessel must be within %s of a friendly shipyard or port before it can be sold.
The vessel must be within %s of a friendly shipyard or port to purchase aircraft.
The radar is cooling down. You must wait %s before using it again.
You are about to activate the vessel\'s radar. It will remain active until you turn it off again, and will expose the vessel to the whole world. Continue?
You are about to activate the radar station\'s radar. It will remain active until you turn it off again, and will expose the structure to the whole world. Continue?
You are about to activate the aircraft\'s radar. It will remain active until you turn it off again, and will expose the aircraft to the whole world. Continue?
The sonar is cooling down. You must wait %s before using it again.
Perform a sonar ping? This will reveal enemy submarines in range, but it will also reveal THIS ship to all players!
Perform a sonar ping? This will reveal enemy submarines in range, but it will also reveal THIS submarine to all players!
You have no available torpedoes in range of this target.
Insufficient fissile material. You need a total of %s.
This torpedo is preparing and cannot yet fire.
You have %1$d aircraft costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d tanks costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d infantries costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d cargo trucks costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d ships costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d submarines costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d ICBMs costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d missiles costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d interceptors costing %2$s per hour.
You have %1$d torpedoes costing %2$s per hour.
You are paying %1$s alliance tax per hour.

Welcome to Counterforce! We are glad to have you in the community. Counterforce is a strategy game designed to simulate life-like geopolitics including land, air, and naval warfare, resource extraction, trade, consumption, and politics, all using real life GPS coordinates! Your mission is to aid in rebuilding the world after nuclear catastrophe. Do this by building cities. Whoever increases their number of cities the most each week is declared the winner for that week. Visit the BUILD menu to build your first city for free!
Tap This Button To Go To The Build Menu:
Once you build your first city, it will provide you with labor, which is used by extractors to produce resources. See the City Help Guide page for more information.

Wealth generation 
Structure-based combat 
Tank combat 
Air combat 
Naval warfare 
Infantry combat 
Resource production 
Transportation 
Resource refinement 
Alliance politics

The main menu is the screen you will see when you join the game. It includes the map, a top UI bar, and two rows of buttons along the bottom of the screen. The bottom buttons each take you to important screens. (Which you may already know. You are here at the game guide, after all!) 

Tap on the top bar to find a number of useful features. Tap the wealth box to be taken to an overview of your income and expenses, an inventory of all resources you currently possess, a list of all your structures, and more. 

If you are currently damaged, you can tap the health box to the right of the wealth readout to be given the option to heal yourself. 

The box to the right of the health readout shows your ping on the game server. 

Finally, in the top right is a small icon. Tap it and you will be shown a drop-down menu with settings for what icons should show up on the map, as well as a rectangle-selection tool that allows you to select many entities at once. 

That about sums it up for the main menu. Be sure to explore by tapping on various buttons. Most UI features are self-explanatory. Don't be afraid to ask for help, and keep your eye out for question mark buttons throughout the UI screens. They contain helpful information about the screens they are on.

Many types of resources are available for you to obtain and to use. Resources are divided into "tiers" based on how many other resources are required to produce them. Tier 1 resources (lumber, coal, oil, crops, etc) are produced using extractor structures. Build extractors in the build menu. Extractors require labor resources to operate, which are obtained from cities. Once you have tier 1 resources, you can use processors to transform them into more advanced resource types. (Pictured above are an extractor, top, and a processor, bottom.)

Alliances are a key part of gameplay. You can access information about alliances from the globe-icon button on the bottom-right of the main menu. Alliances allow you to form teams with other players. Each alliance can have an unlimited number of players. Players in alliances cannot attack each other. Players can see the units and structures of all the other players in their alliance. Your allies and their structures and units will appear cyan on the map. 

Alliances have taxation. Leaders can customize the tax rate between 15% and 90%. All members of an alliance are taxed on certain forms of income, such as hourly income and picking money up off the ground. All tax revenue is deposited into the alliance coffer. Alliance leaders can withdraw from the alliance coffer as they please, as long as the alliance is not at war and they are not in combat. 

Alliances can also form relationships with each other. "Affiliation" is a peace treaty that prevents alliances from attacking each other, just like being in an alliance with someone else. Units and structures owned by affiliated alliances show up as blue on the map. 

Alliance leaders can also declare war on other alliances they are currently not affiliated with. Wars can only be won when one side surrenders or when one side is totally destroyed. When an alliance loses a war, the money in the alliance coffer is given to the victorious alliances or whichever player gets the final kill that disbands the alliance.
Structures are the basic way players can start playing the game. Structures can be built from the hammer and wrench button on the main menu.
Structures can be turned online and offline. When turned online, the player will be charged the hourly maintenance value for that structure, and the structure will go to \"boot\" mode for %s.

At the end of every hour, the player will be charged for the hourly maintenance of each structure they own. Different structures have a different maintenance cost when online. Offline structures all have an hourly maintenance cost of %s. 
If a player cannot afford the hourly cost of an online structure, the structure will go offline. If the structure is already offline, it will take damage. 

Once structure hit 0 hp, they are destroyed and will disappear.

Combat in Counterforce revolves around projectiles, which includes missiles, torpedoes, artillery shells, and interceptors, and ground uits, including tanks, self-propelled AA guns, and infantry. The concept with each of these is simple: missiles, torpedoes, and artillery shells will fire at a target and when they reach it, they will explode, dealing damage to units and structures in the blast radius. Interceptors are used to shoot down missiles and artillery shells. Main Battle Tanks damage structures and are effective against ground units and structures. Self-propelled AA guns are effective against missiles and aircraft. Infantry can capture structures and are weak against tanks and aircraft. Ground-attack aircraft are highly effective against all grounds units. 
What makes combat more complicated is the types of platforms these weapons can be fired from. 

 Missiles can be fired from missile site structures, ICBM silos, ships, aircraft, players, submarines, and tanks. 

Interceptors can be fired from the same platforms, minus submarines. 

Torpedoes can be fired from ships and submarines. (There are also anti-submarine missiles that can deploy torpedoes when they reach their target.) 

Artillery shells have not been added to the game yet, but will be fired from ships and artillery gun structures. 

It would be too complicated to discuss all of these platforms and give complete information on each weapon on this page alone, so each weapon and platform mentioned here has its own page on this guide.
Counterforce offers many options for offensive operations ranging from land units to air and sea units.

Missile Batteries are the basic offensive option for players. They can be build through the build menu. Missile sites have upgradable reload times and missile capacities. Missile sites have a maximum upgradable slot count of %d. \nMissile Sites can hold ground-launched missiles and nuclear missiles. They cannot hold ICBMs.

ICBM silos are tough structures that are used to store and launch ICBMs. They are not upgradable and have a maximum of 1 slot for a missile. They have high HP and are tough to destroy. Note: Don\'t spend all your money on ICBM silos right away. These structures are considered dangerous, and other players will quickly work to destroy them. In addition, ICBMs themselves are very expensive. You will not be able to afford an ICBM just because you can afford an ICBM silo.

Constructed in the Armory structure, tanks are used to attack structures and other ground units. They are highly effective against infantry, structures including sentry guns, cargo trucks, and other tanks and self-propelled AA guns. Tanks take minimal damage from infantry and sentry guns. Ground attack aircraft, missiles, and artillery are highly effective at killing tanks. They can move around the map, but they will eventually run out of fuel. Tanks will refuel automatically each hour using fuel resources from your inventory. If no fuel is available, they will not refuel. Tanks can slowly cross water, but they will take damage each minute while doing so. \n\nTanks will use metal resources from your inventory at the end of each hour to repair. If no metal is available, they will not repair.

Infantry are purchased in Cities. (See the \"Cities\" help page for information on how to capture cities.) Infantry do not hold missiles. Instead, infantry can be used to capture enemy structures and can fight other infantry units. See the \"Infantry\" help page for complete info on infantry units.

Aircraft are obtained from Airbases. Aircraft are completely customizable by players and can be given numerous features including radar, stealth, missiles, interceptors, and more. See the \"Aircraft\" help page for complete info on aircraft and airbases.

Ships are constructed in Shipyards. (See the \"Shipyards\" help page for information on how to capture shipyards.) Ships are completely customizable by players and can be given numerous features including torpedoes, sonar, radar, missiles, interceptors, aircraft, and more. Ships cannot hold ICBMs. See the \"Ships\" help page for complete info on ships.

Submarines are constructed in Shipyards. (See the \"Shipyards\" help page for information on how to capture shipyards.) Submarines are customizable like ships, but have fewer options. All submarines possess sonar. Submarines can be equipped with missiles, torpedoes, and submarine-launched ICBMs. (SLBMs.) See the \"Submarines\" help page for complete info on submarines.
Artillery guns are rapid firing, short range offensive structures. They can be set to automatically fire artillery shells at a target or within a specified distance of a target location.

As a new player to Counterforce, defenses should be your primary concern. Building missile batteries may be tempting, but you are not going to have the strength to challenge experienced players at the start. Build your defenses first, acquire allies, then worry about offenses. \nFortunately, Counterforce offers many options for players to defend themselves. Mix and match them as you please, or devote yourself to mastering one of them!

SAM batteries are the basic defensive option for players. They can be build through the build menu. SAM sites have upgradable reload times and interceptor capacities. SAM sites have a maximum upgradable slot count of %d. \SAM Sites can hold ground-launched interceptors and nuclear interceptors. They cannot hold ABMs. SAM sites will automatically fire at hostile missiles and aircraft in range.

ABM silos are tough structures that are used to store and launch Anti-Ballistic Interceptors for defense against ICBMs and MIRV warhead. They are not upgradable and have a maximum of 1 slot for an interceptor. They have high HP and are tough to destroy. Note: Don\'t spend all your money on ABM silos right away. ABM interceptors are very expensive. You will not be able to afford an ABM just because you can afford an ABM silo. ABM silos set to \"automatic\" mode will fire at hostile ICBMs and MIRVs in range.

Constructed in the Armory structure, self-propelled AA guns (SPAAGs) essentially functions as mobile sentry guns, except they only target aircraft and missiles. SPAAGs will automatically fire at any missile or GROUND ATTACK aircraft that come within range. They cannot fire at infantry or structures, and are highly vulnerable to main battle tanks, ground attack aircraft, missiles, and artillery. They can move around the map, but they will eventually run out of fuel. If not enough fuel is available, they will not refuel. \n\nTanks will regenerate HP at the end of each hour using metal resources from your inventory. If not enough metal is available, the tank will not regenerate at all.

Aircraft are obtained from Airbases. Aircraft are completely customizable by players and can be given numerous features including radar, stealth, missiles, interceptors, and more. See the \"Aircraft\" help page for complete info on aircraft and airbases.

Ships are constructed in Shipyards. (See the \"Shipyards\" help page for information on how to capture shipyards.) Ships are completely customizable by players and can be given numerous features including torpedoes, sonar, radar, missiles, interceptors, aircraft, and more. Ships cannot hold ICBMs. See the \"Ships\" help page for complete info on ships. Ships equipped with interceptors will automatically fire at hostile missiles and aircraft in range.

Sentry Guns are structures that players can build as a point-defense against missiles ad artillery shells. Sentry guns have a range of %1$s, reload time of %2$s, and an accuracy of %3$s. Sentry Guns can only shoot down aircraft that are strafing or are in kamikaze mode. Sentry guns will also automatically fire at neutral or hostile trucks, tanks, and infantry within range. They are highly vulnerable to tanks, but are highly effective against infantry and cargo trucks.

Missiles are the most prolific form of combat in Counterforce. Missiles come in many varieties with stats based on real-life missiles. You can purchase missiles in missile sites, ships, submarines, tanks, ICBM silos, and even your own player via the \"backpack\" button on the main menu.

Missiles come in many different varieties. In the missile purchase screen, you will see on the right side icons next to each missile option. Tap on these icons for information about what those icons mean. The same icons are displayed below. Tap each one for more info.

Missiles equipped with stealth function just like normal missiles, except enemy missile defences will not automatically fire interceptors to defend against them unless they are within %s from the interceptor launcher (such as a SAM site, ship, or tank.) \n\nNote that the enemy player can still manually fire interceptors!

Missiles equipped with nuclear warheads are the most powerful weapons in Counterforce. They are also one of the few things that require resources to build. Each nuclear missile (and any nuclear weapon) requires a certain amount of Fissile Material resource. The amount needed is displayed underneath the price on the missile purchase screen. 
Nuclear missiles deal extreme damage to anything in their excessive blast radius. Nuclear missiles can also be toggled between ground-burst and air-burst fuzes when you are firing them. 
Air-burst creates an EMP effect, disabling structures, and it also greatly expands the blast radius at the cost of dealing less damage. 
Ground-burst explosions have no EMP effect, have a smaller blast radius, but deal much greater damage. They will also create radiation wherever they detonate. 

Nuclear weapons will likely result in retaliation in-kind, so be very careful before deciding to use one! Nuclear missiles cannot be launched from players.

These are the most powerful weapons in Counterforce, and also the most expensive and difficult to obtain. ICBMs can only be launched from ICBM Silos and submarines. 

ICBMs contain Multiple Independently-targettable Re-entry Vehicles (MIRVs). These are extra warheads that the player can send to different targets from one missile. Each ICBM contains a different number of warheads. Some have just one, while others contain up to 10 warheads. 

ICBMs are unique in that they have to accelerate from 0 velocity at launch to their max speed. When they reach max speed or half of the distance to their target, they will deploy their warheads. Once ICBMs reach their top speed, they are too fast to be intercepted by anything other than ABM interceptors. (These are explained in further detail on the "Interceptors" help page.) 

IMPORTANT NOTE: ICBMs that are travelling slowly CAN BE INTERCEPTED BY NORMAL INTERCEPTORS. When firing an ICBM at a close target, they will have the opportunity to intercept your ICBM with cheap, slow interceptors.

These missiles are designed to destroy submerged submarines that are immune to damage from other missiles. Anti-submarine missiles come in two varieties: nuclear-armed and torpedo-armed. 

Nuclear armed anti-submarine missiles have a bigger blast radius and explode directly on reaching their target location, dealing heavy damage to any submarines in range. 

Torpedo-armed ASMs will deploy a homing torpedo when they reach their target. The torpedo will then function as described on the "Torpedoes" help page, searching for a target to home in on.

Tracking missiles, unlike normal missiles, tracking missiles can follow targets to strike them precisely. They are capable of targetting tanks, trucks, players, and players. \n\nNOTE: Tracking missiles cannot target ships or submarines. They are only capable of tracking land targets.

Anti-ship missiles are tracking missiles that are capable of tracking ships and surfaced submarines. They can also track land targets. They also deal significantly more damage to ships. However, anti-ship missiles are very expensive, so they should be used on the appropriate target for highest efficiency.
These are missiles equipped with electronic countermeasures that reduce interceptor accuracy by %s.

Interceptors are the most common form of defense in Counterforce. Interceptors can be fired from ships, tanks, SAM sites, and ABM silos. Interceptors can also be purchased for your own player via the button on the main menu. Non-nuclear interceptors have an accuracy of %s. \nThe odds of an interceptor destroying a missile successfully is determined by how fast the interceptor is compared to the missile. This includes ICBMs that are in the boost phase still accelerating to their top speed. As long as the ICBM is slower than a given inteceptor, that interceptor can strike the ICBM, making them very useful for short-range ICBM defense.

Interceptors come in ship, air, tank, and ground-launched varieties. Interceptors can be nuclear-armed and they can be classified as anti-ballistic missiles. Once ICBMs reach full speed OR deploy their warheads, ABMs are required to shoot them down.

Nuclear-armed interceptors, like nuclear missiles and torpedoes, require fissile material resources in order to build. The amount needed is listed underneath the interceptor price in the purchase screen. Nuclear interceptors have a 100\% chance of shooting down whatever missile or aircraft they are targetted at. Additionally, they have a 50\% chance of destroying other missiles and aircraft in the blast radius.

Interceptors classified as anti-ballistic are capable of targetting ICBMs and MIRV warheads. They come in nuclear and non-nuclear varieties. Conventional ABMs have the standard interceptor accuracy, while the nuclear variants have a 100\% kill chance against the warheads and ICBMs they are targetting. The area-effect damage that nuclear ABMs have makes them useful for destroying clusters of MIRV warheads as they are deployed.

Torpedoes are specialized anti-naval weapons. Torpedoes deal extreme damage to submarines and ships and come in homing, non-homing, nuclear, and non-nuclear varieties. They are available for purchase only for ships and submarines. Certain missiles can also deploy torpedoes when they reach their target, as noted in the \"Anti-Submarine\" section on the \"Missiles\" help page. Torpedoes have a certain range, and once they have travelled as far as their range allows, they will disappear. If a torpedo strikes land, it will explode. \n\nWarning regarding torpedoes: These are non-discriminating weapons, meaning that if you or your allies sail your ships or submarines near one, it will explode and damage or destroy your vessel.

Like nuclear missiles and interceptors, nuclear torpedoes require fissile material to build. Nuclear torpedoes have an excessive blast radius and can deal high damage to submarines and ships in a large area. Be careful using these, as once again they will not discriminate friend from foe when exploding. Nuclear torpedoes can damage structures if they are in the blast radius, such as if the torpedoes explodes on a coastline.

These torpedoes are capable of tracking a naval target and chasing them down before exploding. When firing a homing torpedo, select an entity or location to send the torpedo towards. When it reaches this area, it will begin circling in the water, looking for a target. It can home in on targets within %s. Torpedoes can randomly lose their target each second, so if you are the target of a torpedo, put distance between it and your ship to give yourself time to escape. \nWARNING: Homing torpedoes do not distinguish friend from foe. Your homing torpedo can and will lock on to friendly ships and submarines if they are available and in-range.

Cities are the foundation of the economy and the main objective in the game. Each week, the player that has INCREASED their number of cities the most will win the weekly championship, earning a special icon for their player on the map and a "victory" stat. (If a tie occurs, the player that is worth the most among those that tied will be given the victory.) Capturing other players' cities as well as building your own both count towards the city increase for the weekly competition. 

Cities produce labor resources each hour which are required for the operation of extractors and processors. 

Your first city is free and is designated as your capital. Your capital city cannot be captured and can be built anywhere. Additional cities will cost an ever-increasing amount of resources and must be built several kilometers away from eachother.

Shipyards are located on the map in their real-life locations for players to travel to to capture. Shipyards are used to design and build ships and submarines. In order to re-arm with weaponry of any kind, ships and submarines must be surfaced and within %1$s of a FRIENDLY shipyard. Ships and submarines near these friendly shipyards will repair at a rate of %2$d HP per hour.

Shipyards, like cities, work like "king of the hill." To capture a shipyard, players must build structures within %s of the shipyard, as denoted by the circle around the shipyard that can be seen when selecting it. 
Once you have built a structure around the shipyard, it becomes yours. This means that you must travel to the shipyard in-person to capture it. Note however that once you build a structure near a shipyard, other players can use infantry to capture your structures, which will give them ownership of the shipyard. 
If there are many structures belonging to different players near the shipyard, the shipyard will revert to neutral status and become "contested", meaning no player gets the benefit of owning it. 
If all structures near a shipyard belong to different players but those players are in an alliance, the player with the most structures will be given ownership, and all their allies nearby will get the benefit as well. 

 Once a player owns the shipyard, they can use it to design and construct ships and submarines. Shipyards stocked with machinery will produced ships and submarines faster.

When a player captures a shipyard, both the player and all their allies have access to use the shipyard to design naval vessels. (Note: Currently, shipyards can only produce one ship at a time. In the future they will be able to produce multiple different types of ships at a time.) 

The design process is fairly self-explanatory via help prompts on the design screen. When designing a vessel, tap on the various customization options to see a description of how they affect the design. 
When designing a ship, each feature you add to the ship increases its displacement. The displacement of a ship determines its price, production time, and HP. 

Once you are done with your design, you will need to pay a design fee in order to save the design for fast future use, or you can purchase a one-off model of the ship. Should you choose to save your design, you can make your design publicly available. Publicly available designs can be purchased by other players, and whenever one of these designs is purchased the designer will receive a profit from the purchase.

[Dev note: It is not clear to me yet what features about ships actually need explaining. You customize them. They can have missiles/interceptors/torpedoes/aircraft/CIWS guns, etc. Not complicated. This page will stay blank until players start asking questions about them, which will give me a better idea of what to say here. -Biscuit]

Submarines are stealthy, lethal weapons that are hard to obtain. They are meant for high-skill players who are comfortable with taking a lot of risk for the potential of great reward. Submarines can be armed with missiles, torpedoes, and submarine-launched ballistic missiles. While submerged, a submarine is only vulnerable to homing torpedoes and anti-submarine missiles. Sonar pings from enemy vessels however can detect submarines. Submarines cannot fire missiles or SLBMs while submerged. Be careful when using submarines: they allow you to potentially bring devastation to entire navies and entire alliances via nuclear weapons, but they are very expensive, and it may only take one cheap torpedo to throw away all that you\'ve invested into one. \n\nNuclear-armed submarines guarantee the ability to retaliate against enemies that destroy your land and surface naval units. They may think they\'ve won, but thanks to your hidden SLBMs, you\'ll get the last laugh.
Infantry units can be used to defend your base, attack other infantry units, or to capture enemy structures, cities, caravans, and trucks. \n\nInfantry units are produced in Cities.

Infantry can be toggled to a "defense" mode, in which they will automatically attack any other infantry that get too close. Use this feature to form a perimeter around your base. Infantry can also be ordered to attack other infantry, or to occupy a location. When they occupy a location, they will capture all structure, cities, and cargo trucks within the red outline around them. Defending infantry have a damage boost against attacking infantry.
Trucks are produced in warehouse structures and are used for transporting resources, missiles, and interceptors.

Resources are used for many practical purposes in Counterforce. They will be consumed automatically by various structures to perform their functions, and unit that need refueling will use the owner\'s fuel resources to refuel. Missiles, aircraft, tanks, trucks, ships, etc, all require resources to build.

To produce resources, you must first prospect for resource deposits. You can find the prospect button in the top of the build menu. The kind of terrain you are currently positioned on determines what types of resource deposits you are likely to find. You can prospect for resources once every 7 days. 
To see what type of terrain you are standing on, tap the map near your player. "Common" resource types have a %1$s chance of spawning. Uncommon types have a %2$s. Uranium has a %3$s chance, except in the desert, where uranium has a %4$s probability. 

Plains: Common types: Crops. 
Forest: Common types: Lumber. 
Mountains: Common types: Ore, Coal, Concrete. 
Desert: Common types: Oil, Saltpeter. Increased chance to find Uranium.

Resources come in many varieties and are used for different purposes. 
Crops: Used to produce food and medicine. 
Ore: Used to produce metal. 
Coal: Used to produce electricity, metal, and explosives. 
Saltpeter: Used to produce explosives and nerve agent. 
Oil: Used to produce machinery, fuel, and nerve agent, and consumed to refuel ships and submarines at the end of each hour. 
Metal: Used in the production of machinery, as well as to purchase aircraft, missiles, tanks, trucks, and ships. 
Electricity: Boosts the boot time of structures significantly. Provides power to railguns. 
Uranium: Used to produce fissile material. 
Concrete: Used to produce construction supplies and used in the construction of several processing structures. 
Lumber: Used to produce construction supplies and used in the construction of several processing structures. 
Food: Used to pay the hourly upkeep cost for infantry and is used in the production of medicine. 
Fuel: Needed for hourly tank and cargo truck fuel upkeep and refuels aircraft at the end of each hour. 
Explosives: Used to produce missiles. 
Nerve Agent: Used to produce chemical weapons. 
Construction Supplies: Used to repair structure HP automatically at the end of each hour. 
Electronics: Used to build advanced missiles and aircraft, purchase laser defenses, and consumed to allow structures to resist EMP effects. 
Medicine: Heals infantry automatically at the end of each hour. 
Machinery: Required in order to build some resource processing structures, as well as to build some advanced missiles, aircraft, and ships. 
Fissile Material: Used to produce electricity, nuclear weapons, and nuclear-powered ships and submarines. 
Gold: Scrapped in the scrap yard for large profits.

Resource extractors can be built anywhere, and will produce resources for you to use, provided you have the necessary labor resources to operate them. Extractors built on their matching terrain type (terrain type can be seen by tapping the map) or built within range of a matching resource deposit (for example, an oil well near a crude oil deposit) will have boosted resource output. Resource deposits give a big boost to extractors built near them. 

Terrain Matches: 
Ore Mine -> Mountains 
Coal Mine -> Mountains 
SaltPeter Mine -> Desert 
Lumber Mill -> Forest 
Quarry -> Mountains, Plains 
Farm -> Plains 
Oil Well -> Desert 
Solar Panel -> Plains, Desert 

NOTE: Gold and uranium mines do not benefit from terrain types and do not always output loot each hour. They have a low chance of spawning a resource each hour.

Processors are a class of structure that takes raw material resources and converts them into a new type of resource. They all require labor to operate, and other inputs can be seen on the processor's UI when selected. 

Processors come in several types: 

Granary: Processes crops into food. 
Foundry: Turns ore and coal into metal. 
Oil Refinery: Turns oil into fuel. 
Construction Yard: Turns lumber and concrete into construction supplies. 
Explosives Factory Turns coal and saltpeter into explosives. 
Chemical Plant: Turns oil and saltpeter into nerve agent. 
Laboratory: Turns food into medicine. 
Machine Shop: Turns metal and oil into machinery. 
Lithography Plant: Converts electricity and machinery into electronics. 
Power Plant: Turns coal into electricity. 
Enrichment Facility: Converts uranium into fissile material. 
Nuclear Power Plant: Converts fissile material into significant amounts of electricity.

We have many more features to implement. The following list is a basic roadmap we plan to follow. We may at times add features to this list, develop features slightly out of order, or remove planned features altogether. This list is intended as a rough guide to give players some idea of where we are headed. 

Coming updates: 
 Missile Factories 
 Make all units require resources to purchase 

Allow players to transfer ownership of units to eachother. 
Missile/Interceptor Tanks 
Self-Propelled Howitzers 
ICBM Launch Trucks 
Rail-yards and trains. 
Launch Facilities and Satellites. 
Fishing boat option. 
Add interceptors to helicopters. 
Customizable missiles and interceptors. 
Banks and wealth wire-transfer.
Players can earn experience in-game that allows them to rank up. Every time you rank up, your hourly income increases by %1$s. You can gain or lose experience by performing various actions in-game.
Destroy a ship: 3 xp per ton.
Destroy a submarine: %1$s xp per ton.
Build an aircraft: %1$s xp per ton.
You have %1$d population generating %2$s in tax revenue.
Drop this cargo on the ground?

Available machinery will be used to boost unit production time.

Concrete will be consumed to protect the population of the city from radiation.
Food is needed to feed the population to prevent them from starving to death.
Electronics prevent the happiness level of the city from decreasing when there is not enough food.

Completely delete %1$s? Their player an all structures and units belonging to them will be erased. This action is not reversible. If you screw this up, Biscuit will enslave your entire family. Continue?

Counterforce is a location-based strategy game in which you use missiles, aircraft, and other units to attack other players and defend yourself. Don\'t worry, your privacy is protected. Other players are not able to see where you are; they can only see the structures you have built. IMPORTANT: The game will send you warning notifications when you are under attack. In order to do this, you must have Counterforce App notifications permitted in your phone settings.

The goal of Counterforce is to build cities. Whoever builds the most cities each week will be crowned the champion of the week. (You may also capture the cities of other players to win, or destroy them to set your opponents behind.)
Cities are built in the build menu. To build a city, use this button in the bottom menu:

Structures are built on your player\'s real-life location. Structures cannot be built too close together, so you will need to move to make space. To move your player in-game, you must move around in real life.
The build menu contains more information. For more help, visit the help guide via this button on the bottom menu:
If you have any further questions and to join our awesome game community, use this button to visit the game\'s discord server (you can tap this one right now to join the Discord):
Players who subscribe to support the game financially receive exclusive in-game perks. Message @thebisuitcf on the community discord for more information.

This is not the button to build a structure. This button lets you place a blueprint, which will later become a structure if you move close enough to it. This tool helps players build structures automatically while driving, so that they don\'t have to look at their phone while operating a vehicle. Would you still like to place the blueprint?

This is the build menu. Here you can build structures to use for attacking, defending, and economic purposes. To build a structure, tap the button that displays the structure\'s price. The buttons with the blueprints on them are not for building structures. They are to help players build structures safely while driving. The structure will build on your real-life location. You must be far enough away from other structures in order to build. To get far enough away, yu will need to move in real life. (The bottom menu where you found this button scrolls to the right and left. Scroll to see the rest of the buttons.)

This is the server market. Here you can browse the available listings that other players have for sale. To make a market listing, you will need to transport resources to market locations (the shopping cart icons on the map) using trucks, planes, ships, or by going to these locations in-person in real life.

This is your personal inventory. Here you can see what resources you currently have. The totals here DO NOT include resources stored in your trucks, aircraft, ships, or structures. You can tap on these resources to transfer them to nearby cargo hauling units or markets.

This is your personal Surface-to-Air-Missile (SAM) system, also called a "backpack" by players. You will first need to purchase a SAM, after which you can upgrade it and fill it with interceptor missiles for air defense. The interceptors in your backpack must be fired manually. To build automatic SAM defenses, visit the build menu.

This is your personal cruise-missile system, or "backpack." Here you can purchase a personal missile system for your player, allowing you to launch missiles directly from your location. Personal missile systems cannot hold nuclear weapons, artillery shells, air, ship, or submarine-launched missiles, or ICBMs. For those, you will need to build the necessary structures by visiting the build menu.
This button calls in an airdrop nearby, which will spawn cash for you to collect by visiting in person. You can call an airdrop whenever this button is highlighted.

This is the reports tab. This screen shows you all the important events that have happened recently and while you ere offline. Reports are cleared once per day at minimum, so if you log in and see no reports, it most likely means the reports ere recently cleared.
Welcome to the threats and warnings screen. This page displays threats to you, such as incoming missiles.

This is the in-game chat. Messages typed here will be sent to the appropriate audience (either the global chat or your alliance\'s chat, if you are in an alliance.) Keep your messages appropriate! Messages will also show up on the "Reports" screen for the intended recipients.

This is the prospect button. Tapping it will reveal nearby resource deposits that you can mine for resources either by building an extractor structure in the build menu, or by attacking it with missiles. The extracted resources will drop on the ground near the deposit for you to collect in-person or with trucks. Paid members can also collect resources using helicopters.

This is the alliance list. This lists all the alliances in the game currently, as well as the button where you can create your own alliance. Alliances are groups of players that have joined together as a team.
This is the player list. You can search for players here or sort the list of players by multiple attributes such as wealth and offensive value.

This is the help guide. Here there are many pages of information about various game mechanics organize by topic. As the game is being continuously updated, the information here is subject to change, and some of it may be slightly out-of-date.

This is the settings menu. Here you can filter what icons are visible on the map, change your map theme, upload a player avatar, and toggle various settings. This page also contains links to the game website, a link to email the administrators, and a link to the discord.

This is a shipyard. You can capture it by traveling to it in real life and building a structure inside the circle around it. Shipyards are used to construct custom-designed ships and submarines for naval warfare and for bulk transport of resources and ground units across the ocean. Shipyards also have a cargo bay that is are used to receive cargo from ships so that it can be transferred to ground units or nearby structures. Ships cannot transfer cargo to ground units unless they have the amphibious feature.

This is a port. You can capture it by traveling to it in real life and building a structure inside the circle around it. Ports are used to receive cargo from ships so that it can be transferred to ground units or nearby structures. Ship cannot transfer cargo to ground units unless they have the amphibious feature.

This is a missile. Missiles can have different characteristics that set them apart from eachother. Missile stats are listed at the bottom of the selection box. To destroy a missile, use an interceptor launched from a SAM site, your personal backpack, or use a sentry gun. The faster an interceptor missile is, the better chance it has of shooting down a missile. Some missiles are nuclear, but you can\'t see what kind of warhead they have while in the air!
This is an ICBM. ICBMs can only be intercepted by Anti-Ballistic Missiles, or ABMs for short.

This is an interceptor, a missile used to shoot down other missiles or aircraft. They have a chance of missing their target. The faster the interceptor is compared to its target, the better chance it has to destroy the target.
This is an Anti-Ballistic Missile, or ABM. ABMs are used to interceptor ICBMs and can only be fired from ABM silos.

This is a torpedo. These can be deployed by missiles, dropped by aircraft, or launched by ships and submarines. Homing torpedoes will search for nearby naval vessel to track. Be careful when using homing torpedoes, as they can lock onto friendly ships!

This is a missile battery. These are used to store and launch nuclear and non-nuclear missiles. Missile batteries can be upgraded to hold more missiles and to ire them faster. You can use the target button in the missile battery to select a target for the battery to automatically fire at. ICBMs, artillery shells, and some types of missiles cannot e fired by missile batteries.

ICBM silos hold one missile and are used to launch ICBMs. There will be a launch delay upon firing an ICBM at a target, during which players are warned of an impending launch and your ICBM is vulnerable to attack! They are tough, with lots of HP.

SAM sites are used to automatically intercept enemy missiles and aircraft. Like missile batteries, they can be upgraded to hold more interceptors and to fire them faster. SAM sites cannot intercept ICBMs. You need an ABM silo to intercept them.
ABM silos hold one interceptor and are used to launch ABMs to intercept ICBM warheads. They are tough, with lots of HP.

Sentry guns are used as close-range point defense against missiles, strafing airplanes, and helicopters. They can also attack infantry, tanks, other ground units, and even players that stray too close. Use them around your base to defend against missile and ground attacks. Ground units that are under fire from sentry guns will be slowed down to a crawl.
Artillery guns are close-range offensive structures used for artillery bombardment. Use the target button to set a target for the gun to automatically fire at.

Ore mines, farms, quarries, lumber mills, etc, are called "extractors" and are used to produce resources. Build them on the correct terrain type (see terrain type by tapping the map) or within range of a resource deposit for boosted resoruce output. Paid members can also use helicopters to collect resources.
CommandPosts defend players from radiation damage and missile and aircraft attack. They are tough, with lots of HP.

Warehouses act as gateways to the owner\'s inventory. Your units can transfer resources into your inventory by giving them directly to you or by transferring them into one of your warehouses. Warehouses can be captured by players directly or by infantry. In either case, a portion of the owner\'s inventory resources will be given straight to the capturing player, making warehouse raiding a good way to obtain resources.
Radar Stations are use to detect missiles, aircraft, and ground units so they are visible on the map. Build atleast one radar station to detect these units within several hundred kilometers.

This is loot. Tapping the loot will tell you what kind of resources or missiles it contains. Loot can be collected either by travelling to it in person or by using a truck. Paid members can also use helicopters to collect loot.
This is rubble. You can repair it into the type of structure it was originally using construction supply resources.
This is an airdrop. When the countdown hits zero, it will release loot continuing wealth for you to collect either in-person or with trucks. Paid members can also use helicopters to collect loot.
Airbases are used to store airplanes (and helicopters, for paid members). You can also use airbases to custom-design new aircraft types.
Armories are used to produce tanks, self-propelled anti-aircraft guns, and to store ground units.

This is a processor. Processors are used to convert resource types into more complex resource types. The inputs and outputs on the processor interface will tell you what it needs and what it produces. Coming soon: Processors will level-up over time as they produce resources, allowing them to produce more resources more efficiently. Consider capturing processors instead of destroying them.
Scrap-yards are use to convert resources and ground units into wealth. Load resources or ground units into the scrap yard, and every hour it will convert a portion of them into money.

This is an airplane. Planes are custom-designed by players and purchased in airbases. Planes can be outfitted to carry cargo, missiles, interceptors, guns for strafing ground targets or attacking other flying units, fuel transfer abilities for refueling other aircraft in-flight, airborne radars, and more. When an aircraft design is purchased, the designer gets a kickback.

This is a helicopter. Helicopters can carry missiles, interceptors, and cargo, all at once. They can fly over land or water. They can land on aircraft carriers or in airbases. They are effective against ground targets but are highly vulnerable to air defenses and airplanes. You must be a paid member to get access to aircraft.

Blueprints are not structures, but they will help you build structures automatically when you pass through them. You will still be charged the full price of the structure you want to build. Blueprints are tools used primarily by players who are driving so that they can build structures safely.

Infantry units are purchased in cities and are used for capturing structures or to attack other infantry or players. Infantry do not deal damage to structures, tanks, trucks, etc. They are highly vulnerable against sentry guns, tanks, and aircraft.
Tanks can deal damage against structures, other tanks, and infantry. They are vulnerable against artillery fire, strafing aircraft, and anti-tank missiles.
SPAAGs are mobile air defence assets. They are highly vulnerable against main battle tanks, aircraft, and anti-tank missiles.
Cargo trucks can travel long distances hauling cargo.
Mining trucks are less fuel efficient than cargo trucks, but are significantly tougher and can carry much more cargo.
Resource deposits are regions that contain extra resources of a certain type. Extractors of the right type built within this region will have boosted output.
This is a market. Transfer resources into the market to create a market listing on the server market for other players to purchase. Listings may take up to one hour to appear on the market.

Ships are custom designed by players and purchased in shipyards. They are used primarily as mobile bases or for transporting millions of resources at a time. They can be outfitted with numerous options including cargo, conventional or nuclear-fuel options, hundreds of missiles and interceptors, carrying aircraft, and more.

Submarines are custom designed by players and purchase in shipyards. They cannot carry cargo like ships can, but they can be outfitted to carry torpedoes, missiles, and SLBMs. Submarines can dive, where they cannot fire missiles but are invisible, and they can surface, allowing them to launch missiles or SLBMs. They can also perform sonar scans revealing nearby ships and submarines. When launching an SLBM from a submarine, there will be a launch delay during which the submarine is visible to everyone and vulnerable.
Choose an extractor type. (Long-press to place blueprint.)
Choose a processor type. (Long-press to place blueprint.)
It appears you are logging an account in on a new device under suspicious circumstances. Please continue using the old device or contact admins.

This device already has an account associated with it. Log in using the Google Account already used on this device.
You must build gold mines near gold deposits.
You must play for at least two weeks before you can access the market.
Create a listing on the market? There is a %1$s tax rate to list on the market, so this listing will cost you %2$s. Continue?
You must choose a quantity greater than 0 to purchase. Use the slider to choose how many of this item you would like to buy.
Save this design? This will cost %s.
Caravans cheaply and more slowly transport smaller amounts of materials.
Mints consume labor and gold resources and convert them into wealth.
Cities provide labor resources and allow you to build more resource extractors. \nCurrent extractor budget: %1$d. \nNext extractor budget: %2$d.
You must be at least %s away from any other cities to build another one.
Missile artillery functions as a mobile missile battery.
SAM artillery functions as a mobile SAM battery.
Howitzers function as mobile artillery guns.
You are above %1$d total value. You can only attack other players above %2$d total value.
You are under %1$d total value. You can only attack other players under %2$d total value.
You cannot capture a player\'s capital city.
Build a city for free to start generating labor resources.
Remove this blueprint? This will be completed immediately and cannot be undone.
AREA IS CONTAMINATED. EXPOSURE LETHAL. Expires in: %1$s
Chemical weapons create contaminated areas that instantly kill players and infantry passing through them as well as preventing any structures inside them from being brought online.
This structure is contaminated by chemical weapons and cannot be brought online.
Some of your structures are contaminated by chemical weapons and cannot be brought online.
Railguns fire high velocity projectiles at unlimited distances. Each shot requires electricity and damages the railgun slightly.
EMP weapons have a chance to reboot structures inside their blast radius.
You must reach level %d before you can use the market.
The selected ship cannot hold aircraft.
Open this airbase? This will allow ANY other player to land aircraft here.
Close this airbase? This will prevent any other player, even allies, from landing here.
Aircraft factories allow players to build aircraft to send to their airbases. Aircraft cannot land at aircraft factories.
Capturing this aircraft cannot be undone and may provoke retaliation. Continue?
To purchase aircraft, build an aircraft factory or coordinate with other players who are selling aircraft.
Markets allow players to list items on the server market. Build near a warehouse for best results!
You are a prisoner of this alliance and cannot leave. You must wait until the weekly reset.
This will cancel your affiliation offer to $1%s. Continue?
Blacklisting %1$s will cause your SAMs to automatically shoot down their aircraft. Continue?
Whitelisting %1$s will allow their aircraft to pass through your airspace without being intercepted by your SAMs. Continue?
You have reached your max extractor limit. Build another city to construct more extractors.
Build cities to increase extractor budget.
Your extractor count exceeds what your cities allow. Excess extractors will take damage.
Upgrade to the maximum slot count of %1$d? This will cost %2$s.
You cannot add any more sentries to this ship. You must increase tonnage first.
You must be a member to change the aircraft\'s icon.
This player is not a prisoner. You cannot liberate them.
The original owner of this structure is AWOL. It cannot be returned to them.
This structure is not captured. It cannot be liberated.
Frigates are light vessels that feature radar, missiles, and interceptors.
Destroyers are formidable combatants that feature larger radar and more missiles and interceptors.
Assault Ships carry aircraft and can deploy ground units without a port or shipyard.
Cargo Ships haul cargo and ground units between ports and shipyards.
Fleet Oilers allow nearby friendly vessels to refuel.
Aircraft Carriers are large nuclear-powered vessels that can carry strike fighters.
Attack submarines are light combatants that carry torpedoes and missiles.
Ballistic Missile Submarines are nuclear-powered vessels that can carry nuclear SLBMs.
Watch towers defend areas against ground units such as trucks, tanks, and infantry.
Tap here to turn on app notifications to receive early warning of inbound attacks.
